macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stocks Rebound Sharply After Strong Economic Data and Easing Oil Prices
items.summary: The S&P 500 rose 0.8% to 6,869.50, recovering most losses since the war with Iran began; Dow climbed 238 points or 0.5% to 48,739.41, and Nasdaq gained 1.3% to 22,807.48 amid positive economic updates and lower oil prices.

items.title: Ross Stores Surges 8% on Strong Q4 Earnings and Positive 2026 Outlook
items.summary: Ross Stores shares climbed 8% after reporting better-than-expected quarterly profit and revenue, stating entry into 2026 with solid momentum; Expedia Group rose 3.1%.

items.title: Big Tech Leads Market Rally with Amazon Up 3.9% and Nvidia +1.7%
items.summary: Amazon gained 3.9% and Nvidia added 1.7%, driving the S&P 500 higher due to their significant market weight amid rebound from geopolitical tensions.

items.title: 10-Year Treasury Yield Rises to 4.09% Amid Inflation Concerns from Oil Spike
items.summary: Treasury yields increased with the 10-year at 4.09% from 4.06%, complicating Fed's efforts to manage inflation heightened by oil price jumps tied to Iran war.

items.title: South Korea's Kospi Plunges 12.1% in Historic Worst Day on War Uncertainty
items.summary: South Korea’s Kospi index fell 12.1%—its worst loss ever—early Wednesday due to uncertainty from the war with Iran, influencing global market volatility.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Trump Pressures Banks to Support Crypto Legislation
items.summary: President Donald Trump urged Wall Street banks to stop hindering cryptocurrency legislation and accept a 'Crypto Agenda,' pushing for acts like The Clarity Act to keep digital asset innovation in the US. Bitcoin initially reacted positively but later pulled back amid market uncertainty.

items.title: Binance Partners with ONDO Finance for Tokenized Stocks
items.summary: Binance announced a major partnership with ONDO Finance to offer 24/7 tokenized stocks and real world assets to millions of crypto users, potentially catalyzing the RWA narrative and mainstream adoption.

items.title: Analysts Predict End of Crypto Winter and Bitcoin Upside to $74K
items.summary: Market analysts noted Bitcoin finding support and recovering, with potential upside to $74,000 levels, alongside signals that the crypto winter may be ending, boosting risk-on sentiment.

items.title: Fundstrat Sees March Gains Led by Crypto and Tech Stocks
items.summary: Tom Lee from Fundstrat indicated March could be an up month for markets, driven by MAG7, software, and crypto, with signs the crypto winter is ending despite geopolitical tensions.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Nvidia Releases Cosmos and GR00T Models for Robot Learning
items.summary: Nvidia launched new open models Cosmos and GR00T for robot learning and reasoning, along with the energy-efficient Blackwell-powered Jetson T4000 module. Arm established a new Physical AI business unit, while Siemens and Nvidia announced an Industrial AI Operating System.

items.title: Huawei Unveils AI Data Platform at MWC Barcelona 2026
items.summary: Huawei launched its AI Data Platform to accelerate enterprise AI adoption, featuring real-time multimodal knowledge retrieval, KV cache for inference acceleration, and memory extraction for smarter agents. The platform addresses challenges in scaling AI agents for production use.

items.title: Alibaba Enters Physical AI Race with RynnBrain Open-Source Model
items.summary: Alibaba released RynnBrain, an open-source AI model for robots to comprehend the physical world and identify objects, joining Nvidia's Cosmos and Google DeepMind's Gemini Robotics. China's humanoid robot manufacturing scales rapidly with over 140 manufacturers.

items.title: AI Evolution in Energy Materials Research Outlined in New Study
items.summary: A Tongji University study maps AI's progression in energy materials from machine learning to large models, accelerating discoveries in batteries and electrocatalysis. It highlights potential for self-driving laboratories to autonomously design and analyze experiments.

items.title: Huawei Pushes 5G-A and AI-Centric Networks for 6G Transition
items.summary: Huawei's CEO emphasized 5G-A's role in enabling AI services with ultra-high bandwidth and low-latency, paving the way for 6G by 2029. The company is developing AI-Centric Network solutions for autonomous networks.

items.title: ZTE Launches AIR MAX Solution for AI-Era Mobile Networks
items.summary: At MWC Barcelona 2026, ZTE introduced the AIR MAX solution to optimize mobile networks for AI with enhanced efficiency and integration.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Crimson Desert Gameplay Previews Released Amid Review Drama
items.summary: Pearl Abyss's Crimson Desert, set for release on March 19, 2026, for PS5, Xbox, PC, and macOS, saw its preview embargo lift with extensive gameplay footage of boss fights and the open-world of Pywel. The marketing director addressed review drama, affirming transparency.[2][3]

items.title: Highguard Shuts Down After Just Three Months
items.summary: Wildlife Entertainment announced the shutdown of Highguard on March 12, 2026, after its January 26 launch failed to sustain player numbers despite initial millions, leading to layoffs.[3]

items.title: Bungie Reveals Marathon Seasons 1-3 Details
items.summary: Bungie unveiled plans for Marathon's first year post its March 5, 2026 release, including new weapons, a runner shell, Dire Marsh map with night gameplay, and ranked mode launching mid-March.[4]

items.title: Sony Scales Back PC Ports for PlayStation Exclusives
items.summary: Sony is reportedly reducing PC releases of its PlayStation exclusives like Sorrowos, with exceptions for published titles such as Death Stranding 2.[3]

items.title: Capcom Reports Resident Evil Requiem as Fastest-Selling Horror Hit
items.summary: Resident Evil Requiem became Capcom's quickest-selling horror title, with the company noting PC sales now dominate and expecting further growth.[3]

items.title: Multiple 2026 Horror and Action Games Announced
items.summary: A roundup highlighted upcoming titles like Directive 8020 (May 12), Cthulhu The Cosmic Abyss (April 16), Judas (TBA), and others built on Unreal Engine 5 for PS5, Xbox, PC, and Switch.[1]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Unveils MacBook Neo, New MacBook Air, Pros, and Displays at March 2026 Event
items.summary: Apple launched the $599 MacBook Neo, MacBook Air with M5 chip, MacBook Pros with M5 Pro and M5 Max chips, new Apple Studio Displays, following earlier iPhone 17e and M4 iPad Air announcements. The event features internal upgrades like faster M5 processors touted for performance gains.

items.title: BYD Announces March 5 Tech Event with 1,000+ km EV Range and Megawatt Flash Charging
items.summary: BYD will unveil technologies including a pure EV with over 1,000 km CLTC range via second-generation Blade Battery, Megawatt Flash Charging 2.0, next-gen DM 6.0 hybrid platform, and DiPilot 5.0 driver assistance system.

items.title: TELUS Partners with AST SpaceMobile for Space-Based Cellular Broadband in Canada
items.summary: TELUS and AST SpaceMobile signed a deal to deliver space-based cellular broadband accessible by standard smartphones to remote areas across Canada, enhancing 5G+ and LTE coverage.

items.title: Trump Secures Big Tech Pledge to Cover Data Center Power Costs
items.summary: President Donald Trump obtained a commitment from Big Tech companies to bear the costs of power for data centers amid rising energy demands.
